summary: P0 Multi-Specialty Support Testing Complete - All 13 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified for multi-select specialty checkboxes, +N badge display, and primary specialty selection.

minor.issue: Login response does not include multi-specialty fields (specialty_ids, specialties, primary_specialty_id) - uses legacy fields for backwards compatibility. Full data available via /api/users endpoint.

passed_tests: test_01_create_user_with_multiple_specialties - API accepts specialty_ids array and returns full multi-specialty response
test_02_create_user_with_legacy_single_specialty - Backwards compatibility: specialty_id converted to specialty_ids array
test_03_edit_user_add_specialties - User can be updated from 1 to 3 specialties
test_04_edit_user_remove_specialties - User can be updated from 3 to 1 specialty, primary auto-adjusts
test_05_primary_specialty_validation - API rejects primary_specialty_id not in specialty_ids
test_06_at_least_one_specialty_required - API requires at least one specialty
test_08_user_list_shows_multiple_specialties - User list returns specialty_ids, specialties, primary_specialty_id
test_09_existing_multi_specialty_user - Multi-Specialty Test User has 3 specialties verified
test_10_pool_routing_query_uses_or_for_specialty_ids - Pool routing uses $or query for ANY specialty match
test_11_user_with_multiple_specialties_matches_any - User with multiple specialties can match tickets for ANY specialty
test_12_api_accepts_specialty_ids_array - API correctly processes specialty_ids from frontend
test_13_api_returns_specialties_with_is_primary_flag - API returns specialties array with is_primary flag
frontend_tests_passed: IAM page loads with Users tab showing multi-specialty badges
User list shows +N badge for users with multiple specialties (e.g., '3D Artist', 'Account Executive', '+1 more')
Add User dialog shows 'Specialties * (Select multiple)' label
Multi-select specialty checkboxes work - can select multiple specialties
Primary badge shows on selected primary specialty
Set Primary badges show on other selected specialties
Selection indicator shows 'Selected: N specialties • Primary: [name]'
Edit User dialog pre-selects existing user's specialties
Edit User dialog shows correct primary specialty
critical_code_review_comments: UserCreate and UserUpdate models correctly support both specialty_ids (new) and specialty_id (legacy) fields
build_user_response() helper correctly builds specialties array with is_primary flag
Pool routing in determine_pool_routing() uses $or query: {'$or': [{'specialty_ids': routing_specialty_id}, {'specialty_id': routing_specialty_id}]}
Migration endpoint correctly converts users from single specialty_id to specialty_ids array
Frontend IAMPage.js correctly implements multi-select checkboxes with Primary badge functionality
Legacy fields (specialty_id, specialty_name) maintained for backwards compatibility
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_multi_specialty_support.py - New comprehensive test file for multi-specialty support
seed_data_creation: Multi-Specialty Test User (multispec@test.com) exists with 3 specialties: 3D Artist, Account Executive, Account Manager. Primary: Account Executive.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-specialty support is fully implemented and tested. Users can have multiple specialties via specialty_ids array. Pool routing uses $or query to match ANY specialty in user's specialty_ids. Migration endpoint available at POST /api/users/migrate/single-to-multi-specialty. Frontend shows multi-select checkboxes with Primary badge functionality.

create_user_with_multiple_specialties.validation: At least one specialty required, primary must be in specialty_ids

edit_user_specialties.behavior: Primary auto-adjusts when removed from specialty_ids

legacy_backwards_compatibility.behavior: specialty_id field still accepted, converted to specialty_ids array

user_edit_dialog.ui: Multi-select checkboxes with Primary/Set Primary badges

pool_routing.query: $or query matches ANY specialty in user's specialty_ids array

mocked_apis.note: GoHighLevel payment webhook is MOCKED as per previous context
